cards.title: Anthropic's Trump Feud May Boost Business
cards.body: Anthropic's popularity with business users is growing despite recent government tensions. Sales data suggests the conflict might actually enhance the company's market position.

cards.title: SpaceX Valuation Hits $2.6 Trillion
cards.body: SpaceX's valuation surged by $1 trillion following its IPO launch on Friday. The company briefly exceeded Amazon's market value.

cards.title: Google Launches Android 17 with Gemini
cards.body: Google released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with expanded multitasking and parental controls. A Pixel Drop brings latest AI models to devices.

cards.title: Consumers Turn Off by AI Messaging
cards.body: A WordPress VIP survey finds 60% of US consumers view 'AI' in brand messaging negatively. Companies face backlash despite viewing AI search as key referral channel.

cards.title: SpaceX Goes Public: Complete IPO Guide
cards.body: TechCrunch covers SpaceX's IPO including major winners, pre-IPO deals, and S-1 filing details. The coverage tracks SpaceX's evolution from startup to public company.

cards.title: DOJ Defends xAI's Unpermitted Gas Turbines
cards.body: The Justice Department claims xAI's gas turbines are critical for national and energy security. The Pentagon reportedly needs the infrastructure to continue operations.

cards.title: Plaud Hits $100M ARR with AI Notetakers
cards.body: Plaud reached $100M in annual recurring revenue after shipping over 2M AI meeting notetakers. The company is competing in a crowded AI transcription market.

cards.title: Robinhood Layoffs Skip AI Excuses
cards.body: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev announced 10% layoffs without mentioning AI restructuring. This contrasts sharply with tech peers using AI to justify job cuts.

cards.title: Probably Raises $9M Against AI Hallucinations
cards.body: Probably secured $9M to prevent AI hallucinations and factual errors in outputs. The startup aims to match deterministic systems' reliability standards.

cards.title: SpaceX Acquires Cursor for $60 Billion
cards.body: SpaceX announced a $60B stock acquisition of Cursor days after its IPO. The deal targets SpaceX's struggling AI division amid a $26T addressable market.

cards.title: ChatGPT Market Share Falls Below 50%
cards.body: ChatGPT's dominance slipped below 50% for the first time despite 1.1B monthly users. Gemini follows with 662M users, Claude with 245M.

cards.title: Malaysia's Respond.io Raises $62.5M
cards.body: Respond.io secured $62.5M to expand its AI agent-powered customer service platform. The startup charges per conversation rather than per seat.

cards.title: Google CEO Faced Protests at Stanford
cards.body: Sundar Pichai encountered boos and a walkout at Stanford graduation over Google's Israel and ICE ties. AI's role in defense contracts sparked the controversy.

cards.title: Anthropic Model Ban Signals Government Control
cards.body: The Trump administration's ban on Anthropic's cybersecurity models may be retaliatory or reactionary. The action demonstrates government interference in the AI industry.

cards.title: Meta Launches AI Mode on Facebook
cards.body: Meta rolled out new AI features on Facebook pulling from its cross-platform data. The move reflects Meta's effort to catch up in the AI race.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Experts Protest Anthropic Ban
cards.body: Dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to lift restrictions on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. They argue the ban weakens software security defenses.

cards.title: Salesforce Acquires Fin for $3.6B
cards.body: Salesforce acquired AI customer service platform Fin to enhance Agentforce capabilities. The acquisition strengthens Salesforce's enterprise AI agent offering.

cards.title: Sarvam Becomes India's Latest AI Unicorn
cards.body: HCLTech led a $234M funding round for Indian AI startup Sarvam. HCLTech invested $150M as the company achieves unicorn status.

cards.title: NewCore Raises $66M for AI Agent Security
cards.body: NewCore emerged with $66M to manage AI agents as they become enterprise employees. The startup addresses emerging security challenges in AI workforce management.

cards.title: Satellite Learns to Find Targets Autonomously
cards.body: For the first time, an Earth observation satellite independently located its target in April. The achievement signals a major advance in autonomous satellite technology.

cards.title: Anthropic's popularity surges despite Trump feud
cards.body: Anthropic's business users are growing rapidly, with data from Ramp suggesting the company's popularity is actually increasing. The latest government beef may paradoxically boost the AI company's appeal to enterprise customers.

cards.title: Android 17 expands Google's Gemini AI features
cards.body: Google released Android 17 with new multitasking tools and parental controls. The launch includes a Pixel Drop bringing Google's latest AI models to devices.

cards.title: 60% of US consumers distrust AI in marketing
cards.body: A WordPress VIP survey reveals consumers are wary of AI-generated answers. This skepticism contradicts companies' push to use AI search as a referral channel.

cards.title: Probably raises $9M for reliable AI systems
cards.body: Probably aims to prevent hallucinations and factual errors in AI outputs. The startup seeks accuracy matching traditional deterministic systems.

cards.title: SpaceX acquires Cursor for $60B post-IPO
cards.body: SpaceX is buying AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion to boost its struggling AI division. The company sees a $26 trillion addressable market in AI.

cards.title: ChatGPT loses majority market share for first time
cards.body: ChatGPT's market share slipped below 50% but remains the most popular AI assistant with 1.1 billion monthly users. Gemini has 662 million users while Claude has 245 million.

cards.title: Malaysia's Respond.io raises $62.5M for AI agents
cards.body: Respond.io uses AI agents to handle high-volume customer inquiries with per-conversation pricing instead of per-seat. The startup is now eyes acquisitions.

cards.title: Google's AI used in Israel, ICE ties spark protest
cards.body: Sundar Pichai faced protests and a walkout at Stanford graduation over Google's AI use in defense contracts. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about AI weaponization.

cards.title: Trump admin bans Anthropic's powerful AI models
cards.body: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull its Fable and Mythos cybersecurity models. The decision shows the AI industry remains vulnerable to government interference.

cards.title: Cybersecurity experts protest Anthropic model ban
cards.body: Dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export restrictions on Anthropic's advanced models. They argue the ban limits defenders' ability to secure software.

cards.title: Meta launches 'AI Mode' on Facebook platform
cards.body: Meta rolled out new AI features on Facebook that pull from public info across its platforms. The move aims to catch up in the AI race and boost user engagement.

cards.title: Salesforce acquires Fin for $3.6 billion
cards.body: Salesforce bought AI customer service platform Fin to enhance Agentforce. The acquisition aims to improve Salesforce's enterprise platform for building custom AI agents.

cards.title: Sarvam becomes India's newest AI unicorn
cards.body: Indian startup Sarvam raised $234 million led by HCLTech, which invested $150 million. The company joins the ranks of India's most valuable AI startups.

cards.title: NewCore raises $66M for AI agent security
cards.body: NewCore emerged with $66 million to manage AI agents as enterprise employees. The company argues the next security challenge is managing autonomous AI agents.

cards.title: Earth observation satellite finds targets autonomously
cards.body: In April, an Earth observation satellite located objects on its own for the first time ever. The milestone demonstrates autonomous AI capabilities in space systems.

cards.title: How militaries use AI to make decisions
cards.body: MIT Technology Review compiled six stories about militaries deploying AI models for strategic decision-making. The exclusive eBook covers developments from April 2025 through April 2026.

cards.title: ALS patient becomes brain implant 'power user'
cards.body: Casey Harrell is the first major user of a brain-computer interface that lets him speak despite paralysis. He has used the implanted electrodes for nearly three years.

cards.title: South Koreans embrace AI adoption at scale
cards.body: South Korea demonstrates extreme enthusiasm for AI, from unmanned immigration checkpoints to widespread deployment. The country represents an AI adoption model worth studying globally.

cards.title: Anthropic pauses token-based Claude Agent billing
cards.body: Anthropic announced a pause on token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK. The move signals potential changes to the company's pricing strategy.

cards.title: Pentagon uses AI to write congressional reports
cards.body: The Department of Defense announced it uses AI to write reports mandated by Congress. The practice highlights military adoption of AI automation.

cards.title: OpenAI loses billions annually, financial docs reveal
cards.body: Leaked financial documents show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars each year. The losses raise questions about the company's path to profitability.

cards.title: Mobileye launches standalone US robotaxi service
cards.body: Intel's Mobileye entered the US robotaxi market with its own standalone service. The move marks expansion of autonomous vehicle deployment.

cards.title: Critical Copilot flaw exposed user 2FA codes
cards.body: A critical vulnerability in Copilot allowed hackers to steal two-factor authentication codes from users. The security flaw highlights risks in AI assistant deployment.
